stem: For how many integers \( x \) is \( (x - ([[p]]))(x - ([[q]])) < 0 \)?
solution: The quadratic factors as \( (x-([[p]]))(x-([[q]])) \), which is negative exactly on the open interval \( ([[p]], [[q]]) \). The integers strictly between [[p]] and [[q]] number \( [[q]] - ([[p]]) - 1 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the length of the interval, but counting integers strictly inside requires subtracting 1.
This counts as if both endpoints were included, but the inequality is strict.
This excludes one integer too many.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) with \( [[L]] \le x \le [[U]] \) is \( x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \ge 0 \)?
solution: Factoring, \( x^{2}+([[B]])x+([[C]]) = (x-([[p]]))(x-([[q]])) \), which is negative only on the OPEN interval \( ([[p]], [[q]]) \) (containing \( [[q]]-([[p]])-1 \) integers). So \( \ge 0 \) holds on all other integers in the window: \( ([[U]]-([[L]])+1) - ([[q]]-([[p]])-1) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This removes the CLOSED interval's integer count instead of the open interval's, forgetting the inequality is non-strict so the roots themselves ARE included in the answer.
This reports only the excluded open interval's count, not its complement in the window.
This reports the size of the whole window without removing the excluded interval at all.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) is \( \dfrac{x - ([[p]])}{x - ([[q]])} \le 0 \)?
solution: The quotient is zero at \( x=[[p]] \) (included, since the numerator vanishing satisfies \( \le 0 \)) and undefined at \( x=[[q]] \) (must be excluded). Between them the quotient is negative. The solution set is the half-open interval \( [[[p]], [[q]]) \), which contains \( [[q]]-([[p]]) = [[answer]] \) integers.
distractor_rationales: This treats \( [[q]] \) as included, but the expression is undefined there and must be excluded.
This also excludes \( [[p]] \), but the numerator vanishing there is allowed by \( \le 0 \).
This treats both endpoints as included, ignoring that the denominator's zero must be excluded.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) with \( [[L]] \le x \le [[U]] \) is \( \dfrac{x - ([[p]])}{(x - ([[q]]))^{2}} < 0 \)?
solution: Since \( (x-([[q]]))^{2} > 0 \) whenever it is defined, the quotient has the SAME sign as \( x-([[p]]) \), except that \( x=[[q]] \) must be excluded from the domain (though it does not satisfy the strict inequality anyway). So the quotient is negative exactly when \( x < [[p]] \), giving the integers from [[L]] up to (but not including) [[p]]: a count of \( [[p]] - ([[L]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This mistakenly includes \( x=[[p]] \) itself, but the inequality there equals 0, which fails \( < 0 \).
This excludes one integer too many below \( [[p]] \).
This reports the size of the whole window, forgetting the squared denominator never flips the sign.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) with \( [[L]] \le x \le [[U]] \) is \( (x - ([[p]]))(x - ([[q]]))(x - ([[r]]))> 0 \)?
solution: A sign chart on the cubic \( (x-p)(x-q)(x-r) \) with \( p<q<r \) shows the product is positive exactly on \( (p,q)\cup(r,\infty) \). Within the window, this gives the open interval \( ([[p]],[[q]]) \) (\( [[q]]-([[p]])-1 \) integers) together with the integers strictly above \( [[r]] \) up to \( [[U]] \) (\( [[U]]-[[r]] \) integers), totaling \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This treats the middle interval as closed instead of open (the roots themselves give a zero product, which fails \( >0 \)).
This also treats the upper ray's endpoint at \( [[r]] \) as included, but the product is zero there.
This reports the size of the entire window, ignoring where the product is actually positive.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) with \( [[L]] \le x \le [[U]] \) is \( (x - ([[p]]))^{2}(x - ([[q]])) \ge 0 \)?
solution: The squared factor \( (x-([[p]]))^{2} \) is never negative, so the whole product has the same sign as \( x-([[q]]) \) EXCEPT it also equals 0 (satisfying \( \ge 0 \)) at the isolated repeated root \( x=[[p]] \). So the solution set is \( \{[[p]]\} \cup [[[q]], \infty) \): the single point \( x=[[p]] \) plus the \( [[U]]-[[q]]+1 \) integers from [[q]] to [[U]], totaling \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to include the isolated repeated root \( x=[[p]] \), where the product is exactly zero.
This includes the isolated root but miscounts the upper range by one.
This reports the size of the whole window, ignoring the sign chart entirely.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: The inequality \( x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \le 0 \) has solution set \( [m, M] \) for real numbers \( m \le M \). What is \( m \), the left endpoint?
solution: Since the leading coefficient is positive, the quadratic is \( \le 0 \) exactly between its two roots, so the left endpoint \( m \) is the SMALLER root of \( x^{2}+([[B]])x+([[C]])=0 \): \( m = [[p]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the LARGER root \( M \), the right endpoint, instead of the left one.
This negates the correct left endpoint.
This reads the linear coefficient directly instead of solving for the roots.
This reads the constant term directly instead of solving for the roots.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
